summary: Stunning soundboard capture of a fiery 1988 Greek show, highlighted by a monstrous Let It Grow and soaring Believe It or Not.
review: The recording delivers crystal‑clear soundboard quality with only occasional clipping, letting the band’s high‑energy performance shine. Highlights include an incendiary Let It Grow, emotive Victim > Crazy Fingers sequence, and Jerry’s heart‑breaking vocal on Believe It or Not, while the second set flows smoothly through Space into a blistering Watchtower. Minor quirks such as a flat Throwing Stones and a passable Blackbird keep the show from being flawless, but overall it’s an unforgettable 1988 Greek night.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (average ~4.45) and enthusiastic comments about both performance and sound quality, with only minor technical or musical hiccups noted.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp and stunning; a few reviewers note occasional tape clipping or a clipped drum segment, but overall the mix is clear, balanced and well‑captured.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set opens strongly with Box of Rain, builds through Victim/Crazy Fingers into a soaring Space that feeds the GDTRFB/Watchtower climax and a heartfelt Believe It or Not; segues are occasionally uneven but overall the pacing is engaging and dynamic.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar work is repeatedly praised—soaring solos on Victim, Crazy Fingers, Watchtower and an emotionally charged vocal on Believe It or Not; minor critique of a flat Throwing Stones.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass and vocals receive commendation, especially his duet on Box of Rain and solid grounding throughout the jam sections.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s synth/keyboard textures are highlighted as ethereal and inventive, adding depth to Crazy Fingers, Space and GDTRFB; noted for making the band laugh during Blackbird.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s vibraphone solo and Bill’s drumming are generally praised; a few reviewers mention a clipped drum segment.
